pyiceberg:position-deletes:v2.notes: PyIceberg can read position delete files but only writes copy-on-write deletes (rewrites data files); does not produce position delete files
caveats: Read-only support for position delete files
Write deletes use copy-on-write mode only

pyiceberg:position-deletes:v3.notes: PyIceberg can read V3 deletion vectors (added in v0.10.0) but does not write position delete files

pyiceberg:equality-deletes:v2.notes: PyIceberg can read equality delete files but cannot write them

pyiceberg:equality-deletes:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not support equality deletes in V3
caveats: Equality deletes not supported in any format version

pyiceberg:merge-on-read:v2.notes: PyIceberg can read tables with position and equality delete files (merge-on-read), but cannot write merge-on-read deletes; all deletes use copy-on-write
caveats: Read-side MoR only (position and equality deletes)

pyiceberg:merge-on-read:v3.notes: PyIceberg can read V3 deletion vectors (added in v0.10.0) but cannot write merge-on-read deletes

pyiceberg:copy-on-write:v2.notes: Copy-on-write is PyIceberg's default and only delete/update mode; data files are rewritten without deleted rows

pyiceberg:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Full schema evolution support via update_schema() context manager: add, drop, rename, reorder columns, widen types, and union_by_name

pyiceberg:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Full schema evolution support in V3 via update_schema() context manager

pyiceberg:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values are a V3 feature not applicable to V2

pyiceberg:column-default-values:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not yet support column default values

pyiceberg:table-creation:v2.notes: Full table creation support via catalog.create_table() with Iceberg or PyArrow schemas, partition specs, sort orders, and table properties

pyiceberg:table-creation:v3.notes: V3 table creation supported by setting format-version property to 3

pyiceberg:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: PyIceberg supports hidden partitioning with identity, bucket, truncate, year, month, day, hour transforms; bucket and truncate write support added in v0.9.0 via iceberg-rust

pyiceberg:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Hidden partitioning fully supported in V3 with all transforms including bucket and truncate (via iceberg-rust)

pyiceberg:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Full partition evolution support via update_spec() context manager

pyiceberg:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Partition evolution supported in V3 via update_spec()

pyiceberg:multi-arg-transforms:v2.notes: Multi-argument transforms are a V3 feature not applicable to V2

pyiceberg:multi-arg-transforms:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not yet support multi-argument transforms

pyiceberg:time-travel:v2.notes: Full time travel support via scan(snapshot_id=...) and table.inspect methods with snapshot_id parameter

pyiceberg:table-maintenance:v2.notes: PyIceberg supports expire_snapshots and manage_snapshots APIs; does not support data compaction, manifest rewriting, or orphan file cleanup

pyiceberg:table-maintenance:v3.notes: Same table maintenance limitations as V2 apply to V3

pyiceberg:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Full branching and tagging support via manage_snapshots() API: create_branch, create_tag, with configurable retention policies

pyiceberg:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Branching and tagging fully supported in V3

pyiceberg:read-support:v2.notes: Full read support via scan().to_arrow(), to_pandas(), to_duckdb(), to_ray(); supports row filters, column projection, and streaming via to_arrow_batch_reader()
caveats: Cannot read tables containing equality delete files

pyiceberg:read-support:v3.notes: V3 read support including deletion vectors (added in v0.10.0) and nanosecond timestamps
caveats: Cannot read tables containing equality delete files

pyiceberg:write-insert:v2.notes: Full insert support via append(), overwrite(), and dynamic_partition_overwrite() using PyArrow tables

pyiceberg:write-merge-update-delete:v2.notes: PyIceberg supports delete(filter), overwrite(filter), and upsert() operations via programmatic API; no SQL MERGE/UPDATE/DELETE syntax
caveats: Programmatic API only, no SQL syntax
Deletes use copy-on-write (can be slow for large tables)
Upsert requires identifier fields to be defined

pyiceberg:write-merge-update-delete:v3.notes: Same delete/overwrite/upsert programmatic API available for V3 tables
caveats: Programmatic API only, no SQL syntax

pyiceberg:catalog-integration:v2.notes: Native support for REST, SQL (PostgreSQL, SQLite), Hive, Glue, and DynamoDB catalogs

pyiceberg:catalog-integration:v3.notes: All catalog integrations work with V3 tables

pyiceberg:hive-metastore:v2.notes: Native Hive Metastore catalog support via Thrift protocol

pyiceberg:hive-metastore:v3.notes: Hive Metastore supported for V3 tables

pyiceberg:aws-glue-catalog:v3.notes: AWS Glue Catalog supported for V3 tables

pyiceberg:rest-catalog:v2.notes: Full Iceberg REST Catalog support with OAuth2, SIGv4 authentication, and bearer token signing

pyiceberg:rest-catalog:v3.notes: REST Catalog fully supported for V3 tables

pyiceberg:nessie:v2.notes: No native Nessie catalog implementation; Nessie can be accessed via REST Catalog API when Nessie exposes an Iceberg REST endpoint
caveats: No native NessieCatalog; requires Nessie REST endpoint
Nessie-specific features like branching via Nessie API not directly supported

pyiceberg:nessie:v3.notes: Nessie accessible via REST Catalog API for V3 tables; no native catalog
caveats: No native NessieCatalog; requires Nessie REST endpoint

pyiceberg:polaris:v2.notes: Polaris fully supported as it implements the Iceberg REST Catalog API

pyiceberg:polaris:v3.notes: Polaris supported for V3 tables via REST Catalog API

pyiceberg:unity-catalog:v2.notes: Unity Catalog can be accessed via its Iceberg REST API endpoint
caveats: Requires Unity Catalog Iceberg REST endpoint configuration
Not all Unity Catalog features are accessible via REST API

pyiceberg:unity-catalog:v3.notes: Unity Catalog accessible via REST API for V3 tables
caveats: Requires Unity Catalog Iceberg REST endpoint configuration

pyiceberg:hadoop-catalog:v2.notes: PyIceberg does not implement the Hadoop catalog (JVM-based); use SQL or REST catalog instead

pyiceberg:hadoop-catalog:v3.notes: Hadoop catalog is JVM-only; not applicable to PyIceberg

pyiceberg:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: PyIceberg does not implement JDBC catalog (JVM-based); PyIceberg offers a SQL catalog backed by PostgreSQL or SQLite as an alternative

pyiceberg:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: JDBC catalog is JVM-only; use PyIceberg SQL catalog (PostgreSQL/SQLite) as alternative

pyiceberg:variant-type:v2.notes: Variant type is a V3 feature not applicable to V2

pyiceberg:variant-type:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not yet support the Variant type

pyiceberg:shredded-variant:v2.notes: Shredded variant is a V3 feature not applicable to V2

pyiceberg:shredded-variant:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not yet support shredded variant

pyiceberg:geometry-type:v2.notes: Geometry type is a V3 feature not applicable to V2

pyiceberg:geometry-type:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not yet support geometry types

pyiceberg:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature not applicable to V2

pyiceberg:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: PyIceberg supports timestamp_ns and timestamptz_ns types (added in v0.10.0)

pyiceberg:lineage:v2.notes: Lineage tracking is not supported by PyIceberg

pyiceberg:lineage:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not support lineage tracking

pyiceberg:statistics:v2.notes: PyIceberg reads column statistics from manifest files for scan planning and file pruning. Writes column statistics (min/max, null counts, value counts) when appending or overwriting data. Statistics mode configurable via write.metadata.metrics.default and per-column properties.

pyiceberg:statistics:v3.notes: Column statistics fully supported for V3 tables in PyIceberg

pyiceberg:bloom-filters:v2.notes: PyIceberg does not support reading or writing Parquet-level bloom filters or Puffin-based bloom filter statistics. PyIceberg uses PyArrow for Parquet writing which does not currently respect Iceberg bloom filter table properties.
caveats: PyArrow-based Parquet writer does not support Iceberg bloom filter table properties

pyiceberg:bloom-filters:v3.notes: PyIceberg does not support bloom filters for V3 tables
caveats: PyArrow-based Parquet writer does not support bloom filter configuration

pyiceberg:type-promotion:v2.notes: PyIceberg supports type promotion via the update_schema() context manager's update_column() method. Supports all Iceberg spec-defined promotions: int to long, float to double, and increasing decimal precision. The operation is metadata-only.

pyiceberg:type-promotion:v3.notes: Full type promotion support for V3 tables via update_schema() context manager.

pyiceberg:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v2.notes: PyIceberg has first-class REST catalog support. Configure with type=rest and the Horizon Catalog endpoint in pyiceberg config.

pyiceberg:snowflake-horizon-catalog:v3.notes: PyIceberg REST catalog support is available; V3 feature support depends on PyIceberg version (0.8.0+ recommended).
caveats: Requires PyIceberg 0.8.0+ for V3 table support

pyiceberg:deletion-vectors:v2.notes: Deletion vectors are a V3 feature; not applicable to Iceberg V2

pyiceberg:deletion-vectors:v3.notes: PyIceberg can read V3 deletion vectors (added in v0.10.0) but does not write deletion vectors.
